agents.name: Rex
agents.note: Session 137 -- US-265 SHIPPED passes:true (Sprint 22 discriminator A: tick-thread liveness instrumentation). FOUR artifacts: src/pi/hardware/hardware_manager.py UPDATE (+tickHealthCheckIntervalS constructor parameter default 60.0; +5 state fields _lastTickHealthCheckMono/_lastTickHealthCheckCount/_lastTickHealthCheckOnBattery/_tickHealthAlarmCount + factory wiring; +tickHealthAlarmCount read-only property; +_checkTickThreadHealth helper that snapshots orchestrator.tickCount on a configurable cadence and logs ERROR + increments alarm counter when count unchanged across full window while on BATTERY; loop entry now logs `tid=<id>` + intervalS at INFO; per-iteration health-check call wrapped in try/except so health-check failure CANNOT halt the tick loop per invariant; thread spawn-site log enriched with daemon + ident; factory reads pi.power.tickHealthCheckIntervalS config; mod-history entry; +time module import; ~135 lines net), src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py UPDATE (audit log line in _wirePowerDownOrchestratorCallbacks confirming the orchestrator was instantiated by HardwareManager and the dedicated tick thread spawn is delegated to HardwareManager._startComponents -- pairs symmetrically with hardware_manager `tid=<id>` so a post-Drain-7 forensic walk can prove which side of the boundary failed if the spawn line is missing in journalctl; mod-history entry; ~20 lines net), tests/pi/hardware/test_power_down_tick_thread_health.py NEW (15 tests across 4 classes -- helper-direct discriminator scenarios + property exposure + constructor-param defaults + tick-loop integration including health-check-raising-does-not-kill-loop invariant; ALL 15 would FAIL against pre-fix code per runtime-validation gate -- AttributeError on missing _checkTickThreadHealth + tickHealthAlarmCount, TypeError on missing tickHealthCheckIntervalS kwarg; ~430 lines), tests/pi/power/test_staged_shutdown_actually_fires.py UPDATE (scope-extension: 5-line state-stub addition x3 test methods that bypass __init__ via __new__ and only set bare-minimum attributes the loop reads; my tickHealthCheckIntervalS state addition broke them on the entry log; this is a direct-consequence test update of an API change, not tangential drift -- documented inline). Verification: tests/pi/hardware/test_power_down_tick_thread_health.py 15/15 PASS in 21.87s; pi/{hardware,power,orchestrator,diagnostics,integration} subset 452 passed / 11 skipped / 0 failures in 149.68s (pre-touch was 437 + 3 staged-shutdown regressions; +15 new + 0 regressions); full fast suite 3833 passed / 26 failed / 17 skipped / 19 deselected in 1006.30s (16:46) -- baseline (Session 136 per US-264) was 3818 pass / 26 fail, delta +15 = exactly the new test count + ZERO regressions; the 26 failures unchanged TD-042 + TD-044 set (closed by US-268 + US-269 later this sprint). Ruff clean across all 4 touched files. sprint_lint US-265 OK (only carryover warning is US-263 sizing). Important runtime-validation note: discriminator test test_loopOnBatteryWithNoopTick_raisesAlarm mocks orchestrator.tick at the right level per Sprint 19 lesson (Mock with no side_effect so tickCount stays 0); a no-op tick simulates exactly the hypothesis-A pathology -- thread runs the loop body but counter never advances. Pre-Drain-7 this discriminator surface is now fully wired: when CIO runs Drain Test 7 with US-262 logger active, the journalctl ERROR line `PowerDownOrchestrator tick liveness alarm: tickCount=N unchanged across X.Xs while on BATTERY` will fire 60s after the first health-check window if hypothesis A is the bug; concurrent with the logger CSV's pd_tick_count column staying at 0. If that line does NOT appear in journalctl AND pd_tick_count IS incrementing in the CSV, hypothesis A is RULED OUT and the bug is hypothesis B (US-266) or C (US-267). Pattern carryforward: paired-3-way-symmetry log strategy (lifecycle audit + spawn-site daemon+ident + loop entry tid) lets a post-incident forensic walk in journalctl prove EXACTLY which side of the boundary failed -- reusable for any future cross-module thread-startup instrumentation. Sprint 22 status now: 4/8 shipped (US-262 + US-263 + US-264 + US-265); remaining 4 unclaimed -- US-266 + US-267 discriminator pair (M/S; the other two hypothesis fixes; runtime-validation gate critical for Drain-7 truth-table) + US-268 TD-042 (M; mechanical theme-stub addition across 4 test files; closes 24 TD-042 failures) + US-269 TD-044 (S; one-liner SemVer-shape regex on test_migration_0005). Next-iteration recommended pick: US-266 (M; independent; the discriminator pair lands cleanly together since US-265 covers thread-liveness and US-266 covers gating-logic which are orthogonal in HardwareManager + PowerDownOrchestrator code paths); alternatively US-268 if a quick test-baseline win is preferred (closes the 24 TD-042 failures + brings fast suite to fully-green before discriminator trio completes).
